name: Charlie Kirk
summary: Charlie Kirk's character and pattern of behavior are marked by a consistent promotion of conservative and often extremist views, including opposition to abortion, LGBTQ rights, and racial equality. He has repeatedly spread misinformation and conspiracy theories, particularly regarding COVID-19, voter fraud, and social issues. Kirk has also made racist and xenophobic comments, including claims that Democrats aim to "diminish and decrease white demographics" and that the "Great Replacement" theory is a reality. His actions and statements have been criticized for threatening academic freedom, promoting harassment, and inciting violence, as seen in his involvement in the January 6 US Capitol attack. Overall, Kirk's behavior demonstrates a disregard for facts, a willingness to promote hate and misinformation, and a commitment to extremist ideologies.

entries.fact: Charlie Kirk volunteered for the successful U.S. Senate campaign of Illinois Republican Mark Kirk.
Charlie Kirk co-founded the conservative student organization Turning Point USA (TPUSA), which later expanded into affiliate groups such as Turning Point Action and Turning Point Faith.
Charlie Kirk was rejected from West Point.
Charlie Kirk became a major player in conservative politics, advocating for a range of conservative positions, including opposition to abortion, gun control, DEI programs, and LGBTQ rights.
Charlie Kirk participated in a Fox News event with a pro-Donald Trump tone, alongside Trump Jr., Eric Trump, and Lara Trump.
Turning Point USA's Professor Watchlist was criticized for threatening academic freedom and leading to the targeted harassment of academics.
The Professor Watchlist was briefly suspended by its web host in 2019 due to criticism.
Charlie Kirk's organization Turning Point USA was found to have made misleading financial claims and enriched its leaders while advocating for Trump, according to a ProPublica investigation.
Charlie Kirk's salary from Turning Point USA increased from $27,000 to nearly $300,000, and he bought an $855,000 condo in Longboat Key, Florida.
Charlie Kirk became chairman of Students for Trump, a youth mobilization campaign that had been acquired by Turning Point Action.

Charlie Kirk co-founded the Falkirk Center for Faith and Liberty, a right-wing think tank funded, owned, and housed by Liberty University, which spent at least $50,000 on Facebook advertisements promoting Trump and Republican candidates
Charlie Kirk promoted COVID-19 misinformation and false allegations of electoral fraud in 2020.
Charlie Kirk earned a salary of more than $325,000 from Turning Point USA and related organizations.
Charlie Kirk released his book The MAGA Doctrine, a manifesto for the Make America Great Again movement, in which he described the Republican Party as a 'Trump-remade populist party'.

Charlie Kirk was described by The New York Times as exemplifying 'walking the line between mainstream conservative opinion and outright disinformation' and as having a powerful ally in Donald Trump Jr.
Charlie Kirk stated that Democrats shutting down college campuses would remove opportunities for ballot harvesting and voter fraud, and encouraged keeping campuses closed as a result.

Turning Point USA announced it would launch an online academy as an alternative to schools 'poisoning our youth with anti-American ideas', which was criticized for promoting an 'America-first education'.

Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that Turning Point Action and Students for Trump were sending more than 80 buses of patriots to D.C. to fight for President Trump, the day before the January 6 U.S. Capitol attack
Charlie Kirk's organizations, funded by Publix heiress Julie Fancelli, sent buses to the January 6 event, which led to the storming of the U.S. Capitol
Charlie Kirk paid $60,000 for Kimberly Guilfoyle to speak at the rally that preceded the January 6 U.S. Capitol attack
Ali Alexander blamed Charlie Kirk and TPUSA for financing the travel of demonstrators to the Save America rally, which led to the January 6 U.S. Capitol attack
Turning Point Academy partnered with Dream City Christian School, a private school that received $900,000 in Arizona school voucher funds in the 2022-2023 school year.
Charlie Kirk called for a 'patriot' to bail out of jail the man who broke into Nancy Pelosi's house and attacked and tried to murder her husband with a hammer in a 2022 episode of his podcast.
Charlie Kirk pleaded the Fifth Amendment privilege against self-incrimination when appearing before the U.S. House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack
Charlie Kirk's team provided the U.S. House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack with 8,000 pages of records in response to its requests
Charlie Kirk called for the imprisonment or the death penalty of Joe Biden for 'crimes against America'.

A February 2023 Brookings Institution study found Charlie Kirk's podcast contained the second-highest proportion of false, misleading, and unsubstantiated statements among 36,603 episodes produced by 79 prominent political podcasters.
Charlie Kirk stated that it should be legal to burn a rainbow or Black Lives Matter flag in public.
Charlie Kirk was fatally shot by a rooftop sniper while speaking at a TPUSA debate event at Utah Valley University.
Almost 100,000 people attended Charlie Kirk's memorial service at State Farm Stadium in Glendale, Arizona.
Charlie Kirk alleged that he had lost a slot to attend West Point to a candidate of 'a different ethnicity and gender'.
Charlie Kirk said that universities were 'islands of totalitarianism' and promoted the Cultural Marxism conspiracy theory.
Charlie Kirk promoted debunked claims about George Floyd, including that he was 'illegally counterfeiting currency' and had once 'put a gun to a pregnant woman's stomach'.
Charlie Kirk initially stated that the US should support the separation of church and state.
Charlie Kirk told a college audience that the concept of white privilege is a myth and a 'racist idea'.
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed on social media that a U.S. medical examiner declared George Floyd's death was due to an overdose.
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed that protesters in the French yellow vests movement chanted 'We want Trump'
Charlie Kirk said, 'I believe marriage is one man, one woman', but added that gay people should be allowed in the conservative movement.
Charlie Kirk alleged that the WHO covered up information about the COVID-19 pandemic

Charlie Kirk compared the COVID-19 pandemic to the 2009 swine flu pandemic, falsely stating that it 'took President Barack Obama millions infected and over 1,000 deaths' to declare a public health emergency
Charlie Kirk described social distancing prohibitions in churches as a Democratic plot against Christianity
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed that hydroxychloroquine had proved to be '100% effective in treating' COVID-19
Charlie Kirk made the unfounded assertion that authorities in Wuhan, China, were burning patients
Charlie Kirk refused to abide by mask requirements because 'the science around masks is very questionable'
Charlie Kirk spread false information and conspiracy theories about COVID-19 on social media
Charlie Kirk spread falsehoods about voter fraud ahead of the 2020 U.S. presidential election
Charlie Kirk promoted false and disproven claims of fraud in the 2020 election
Charlie Kirk led a Stop the Steal protest at the Maricopa Tabulation Center in Phoenix

Charlie Kirk told a congregation that the Bible says to 'Occupy until I come', a verse often cited by followers of the Seven Mountain Mandate to assert that evangelical Christians must dominate seven areas of society.
Charlie Kirk promoted misleading claims about the efficacy and safety of COVID-19 vaccines
Charlie Kirk began making anti-LGBTQ remarks, opposing transgender rights and medical care, and asserted that there is an 'LGBTQ agenda'.
Charlie Kirk called the separation of church and state in the US a 'fabrication'.
Charlie Kirk claimed that being gay is an 'error' and likened homosexuality to drug addiction.
Charlie Kirk said that Western civilization is the best that humanity has produced, and that it is an outgrowth of the Bible.
Charlie Kirk stated that some gun deaths are a worthwhile cost to protect the Second Amendment and other God-given rights.
Charlie Kirk argued that abortion is murder and should be illegal, with no exceptions for rape, including for children as young as 10.
Charlie Kirk compared abortion to the Holocaust, and said that abortion is worse.
Charlie Kirk said that the US is living through a constitutional crisis because it is no longer a Christian nation, and that liberty is incompatible with a non-Christian population.
Charlie Kirk stated that the US is a Christian state and should remain that way.
Charlie Kirk argued that abortion is murder and should be illegal, with no exceptions for rape, including for children as young as 10.

Charlie Kirk and Candace Owens presented what the Southern Poverty Law Center described as 'a symphony of racist dog-whistles ... pandering to the extreme right' at the 2018 Western Conservative Summit.
Charlie Kirk asserted that the increasing presence of African Americans in American politics resulted in the Black community being 'worse off'.

Charlie Kirk told followers of his radio podcast that Democratic immigration policies were aimed at 'diminishing and decreasing white demographics in America' and called for Texas to 'deputize a citizen force and put them on the border' to protect 'white demographics in America'.
Charlie Kirk opposed Juneteenth being declared a federal holiday, describing it as 'anti-American' for promoting 'a neo-segregationist view'.

Charlie Kirk wrote in a Fox News article that 'directly confronting the left, and promising to fight their illiberal ideology with state power when necessary, is the key to winning everyday Americans'.
Charlie Kirk began the 'Exposing Critical Racism Tour' to 'fight racist theories on America's college campuses!'
Charlie Kirk said 'the facts are that there are only two genders; that transgenderism and gender 'fluidity' are lies that hurt people and abuse kids' in an op-ed for The American Mind.

Charlie Kirk criticized the Supreme Court's decision in Obergefell v. Hodges, calling LGBTQ activists the 'alphabet mafia' and claiming that the movement is not 'just about two dudes being able to get married'.

Charlie Kirk said at the TPUSA Young Women's Leadership Summit 2022 Conference that the 'biblical model' for women to pursue in romantic relationships is a partner who is 'a protector and a leader, and deep down, a vast majority of you agree'.
Charlie Kirk said that transgender women in women's locker rooms should be 'taken care of the way we used to take care of things in the 1950s and '60s'.

Charlie Kirk said, 'One issue I think that is so against our senses, so against the natural law and, dare I say, a throbbing middle finger to God, is the transgender thing happening in America right now'.
Charlie Kirk said that 'prowling Blacks go around for fun to go target white people' in urban America.

Charlie Kirk reacted to Representative Sheila Jackson Lee's statement on affirmative action by saying, 'If we would have said three weeks ago [...] that Joy Reid and Michelle Obama and Sheila Jackson Lee and Ketanji Brown Jackson were affirmative-action picks, we would have been called racist. But now they're comin' out and they're saying it for us!'
Charlie Kirk described Martin Luther King Jr. as 'awful ... not a good person' and as someone who is admired only because he said 'one thing he didn't actually believe'.
Charlie Kirk condemned the Civil Rights Act of 1964, calling its passage a 'huge mistake' and alleging that it had created a 'permanent DEI-type bureaucracy'.

Charlie Kirk said that a 'myth' had been created around Martin Luther King Jr. which had 'grown totally out of control' and that King was currently 'the most honored, worshiped, even deified person of the 20th century' despite 'most people' supposedly disliking him during his life.
Charlie Kirk called Malcolm Kenyatta's accusation that he was working to undermine King and the Voting Rights Act of 1965 'a lie' and 'fear-mongering'.

Charlie Kirk said that telling the 'truth' about King 'should not be trampling sacred ground' since he was 'just a man ... a very flawed one at that' and a 'mythological anti-racist creation of the 1960s'.
Charlie Kirk said he had 'found the sacred cow of modern America' in criticizing King.
Charlie Kirk blamed DEI programs for national aviation issues, saying 'If I see a Black pilot, I'm going to be like, 'Boy, I hope he's qualified.'

Charlie Kirk's comments about DEI programs and his comment about Black or African American airline pilots resulted in ongoing conflict with the Republican National Committee over outreach to Black voters.
Charlie Kirk called Jackson a 'recipient of affirmative action' and said she was nominated for the Supreme Court because of her race.
Charlie Kirk said, 'The great replacement strategy, which is well under way every single day in our southern border, is a strategy to replace white rural America with something different'.
Charlie Kirk said, 'The American Democrat party hates this country. They wanna see it collapse. They love it when America becomes less white.'

Charlie Kirk posted 'The ' Great Replacement ' is not a theory, it's a reality', alongside a Fox News headline that falsely claimed: '7.2M illegals entered the U.S. under Biden administration, an amount greater than population of 36 states.'
Charlie Kirk called for Trump to propose a nationwide ban of gender-affirming care for transgender people.
Charlie Kirk called for the imprisonment of doctors who perform gender-affirming care and demanded 'Nuremberg-style' trials for them.

Charlie Kirk criticized YouTuber Ms. Rachel for a post that celebrated Pride Month by quoting the Bible verse 'love thy neighbor', arguing that she was being selective and referencing a verse that calls for the death of men who lay with other men.
Charlie Kirk blamed the high death toll of the July 2025 Central Texas floods on DEI.

Charlie Kirk called for the elimination of Jasmine Crockett's congressional district, justifying the erasure of her district by claiming she was part of an 'attempt to eliminate the white population in this country'
Charlie Kirk was a speaker for the annual conference held by the anti-Muslim advocacy group ACT for America in Washington DC
Charlie Kirk repeated pro-Israeli talking points about the ongoing Gaza war and blamed Hamas for the deaths of civilians in Gaza
Charlie Kirk told an audience in Jerusalem that he was 'very pro-Israel' and had defended Israel his whole life

Charlie Kirk stated that 'America does not need more visas for people from India', arguing that the American workforce has become dominated by Indian-American immigrants, effectively decreasing job opportunities for Americans.
Charlie Kirk commented on how race is less important to culture than religion is, stating that America would still be America if it were ethnically 90% Indian, as long as they were Christian Indians.

Charlie Kirk elaborated on Hinduism and his disapproval of its morality due to its polytheism, stating: 'When you have multiple gods, you get different moralities. And the West has largely embraced the idea that there is a standard of conduct, or a best way to live.'

Charlie Kirk responded to an inquiry about how his claim was not inclusive of other religious worldviews, saying 'I don't seek to be inclusive, I seek what is best. And the Ten Commandments are what is best. Would it be offensive to a young Hindu kid? Maybe, maybe not. But it also is a reminder they're living in a country that's a monotheistic country.'

Charlie Kirk argued for the abolition of the Bureau of Indian Affairs, tweeting that it is 'the most mismanaged & inefficient government agency', highlighting that Native Americans are 'the most impoverished American demographic despite receiving the most government benefits'.
Charlie Kirk mentioned alcoholism on reservations, and said that Native Americans had become dependent on government benefits.
Charlie Kirk argued that it's not Islamophobia to notice that Muslims want to import values into the West that seek to destabilize civilization
Charlie Kirk said that immigration to the United States should be completely stopped
Charlie Kirk called for the use of force against migrants at the U.S.–Mexico border, including the use of tear gas, rubber bullets, and whips
Charlie Kirk promoted false claims of disproportionate criminality among migrants, saying they would break into homes and rape women
Charlie Kirk called for Mehdi Hasan to be deported and deplatformed over his views on the COVID-19 pandemic
Charlie Kirk blamed the Chinese government and drug cartels for the opioid crisis in the United States

Charlie Kirk said on The Charlie Kirk Show that 'Jewish donors have been the number 1 funding mechanism of radical, open border, neoliberal, quasi-Marxist policies ... This is a beast created by secular Jews, and now it's coming for Jews'.
Charlie Kirk suggested that Jewish donors control 'not just the colleges; it's the nonprofits, it's the movies, it's Hollywood, it's all of it'.
Charlie Kirk said that 'Jews have been some of the largest funders of cultural Marxist ideas and supporters of those ideas over the last 30 or 40 years.'
Charlie Kirk called on American Jews to stop 'subsidizing your own demise by supporting institutions that breed Anti-Semites and endorse genocidal killers'.
Charlie Kirk defended Elon Musk from charges of antisemitism by claiming the post's charge against Jewish communities was accurate.
Charlie Kirk called U.S. representative Ilhan Omar a 'terrorist sympathizer' and called for her deportation

Charlie Kirk promoted a conspiracy theory alleging the Israeli government knew that Hamas was going to launch the attack and that Benjamin Netanyahu allowed it to go ahead as part of a plan to remain in power
Charlie Kirk promoted the false claim that Haitians in Springfield, Ohio, were eating residents' pets and other wildlife
Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that 'Islam is the sword the left is using to slit the throat of America'
Charlie Kirk posted a tweet that was described as 'gross and Islamophobic' after the victory of Zohran Mamdani in the New York City Democratic mayoral primary
Charlie Kirk opposed U.S. involvement in the Iran–Israel War, warning that a prolonged war would destabilize the region and could trigger a refugee crisis and civil war in Iran
Charlie Kirk opposed a bipartisan bill to expand anti-BDS laws, which punish the boycott of Israel
Charlie Kirk warned his followers against hatred of Jews, calling it 'evil' and 'demonic'
Charlie Kirk said he had a 'bulletproof resumé' showing his defense of Israel and that he would 'fight for' Israel

Charlie Kirk accused Democrats of spreading a 'false narrative' that 'there is a relentless assault against Black people on behalf of white people', and said 'White individuals are actually more likely to be attacked, especially even per capita, by Black individuals in this country.'
Charlie Kirk opposed the Paris Agreement on climate change
Charlie Kirk promoted climate change denial, calling global warming a hoax
Charlie Kirk admitted that Turning Point USA accepted funding from the fossil fuel industry

Charlie Kirk's organization, Turning Point USA, was criticized for its 'Professor Watchlist', which targeted professors for their political views, leading to death threats and harassment against them.
Charlie Kirk stated that there is a separation of church and state and that it should be supported.
Turning Point USA's 'Professor Watchlist' was suspended by its web host due to its controversial nature and potential to incite harassment against professors.
An investigation found that Turning Point USA, a charity founded by Charlie Kirk, had questionable financial reporting and insiders were cashing in.
The Falkirk Center, a think tank co-founded by Charlie Kirk, pushed the boundaries on political advertising and messaging.
A Turning Point USA video featuring Charlie Kirk claimed there was no factual data to back up global warming, which was later rated inaccurate by Science Feedback
Charlie Kirk said on his podcast that there is no separation of church and state, calling it a 'fabrication' and 'fiction' made up by secular humanists.

Charlie Kirk deleted a tweet that claimed he had sent '80+ buses full of patriots' to Washington D.C., which was seen as an attempt to downplay his organization's role in the January 6th Capitol riot.
Charlie Kirk's organization, Turning Point USA, was criticized for its role in the January 6th Capitol riot, with some accusing the group of helping to incite the violence.
Charlie Kirk left the Falkirk Center, a think tank he co-founded with Jerry Falwell Jr. at Liberty University.
Charlie Kirk's organization, Turning Point USA, was criticized for its 'National Watchlist' of school boards that it deemed to be promoting 'radical left' policies.
Charlie Kirk warned that climate activism would erode American sovereignty and private property, describing it as a Trojan Horse for Marxism
Charlie Kirk called the statement that climate change is an existential threat 'complete gibberish nonsense' and stated that he did not believe human activity is the driver of climate change
Charlie Kirk said that the southern border matters more than the Ukrainian border and called for Republican leaders to label the situation at the southern border an invasion
Charlie Kirk advanced pro-Russian talking points about the Russo-Ukrainian War, characterizing the tensions as a 'border dispute' and repeating false claims from Russian state media
Charlie Kirk said that the southern border matters more than the Ukrainian border and called for Republican leaders to label the situation at the southern border an invasion
Charlie Kirk and Kelli Ward, the chair of the Arizona Republican Party, had little to say to January 6th investigators about their efforts to overturn the 2020 election results.
Documents showed that a Publix heiress was willing to spend $3 million on a January 6th rally, which was organized in part by Charlie Kirk's organization, Turning Point USA.

Charlie Kirk offered an 'apology' to the Russian people, stating that very few Americans want war with Russia and that the people obsessed with fighting Russia forever were a minority on their way out of power
Charlie Kirk called Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy a 'CIA puppet' and 'gangster' who 'sent his own people to a senseless massacre'
Charlie Kirk stated that if the US took Taiwan, it would probably start a nuclear war and that US leaders have mishandled China
Charlie Kirk disagreed with Trump's decision to send more military aid to Ukraine, saying it would not lead to a peace settlement
Charlie Kirk sent a letter to Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warning that the country was 'losing the information war' among young Americans
Charlie Kirk floated a conspiracy theory that Netanyahu issued a 'stand down' order as a means to bolster his own political standing after the October 7th attacks.
President Trump called for prayers for Charlie Kirk on Truth Social after the shooting
President Trump and other prominent Republicans immediately blamed Democrats and liberal beliefs for Charlie Kirk's shooting without evidence
Far-right activists, including Laura Loomer, called for violence and retaliation in the aftermath of Charlie Kirk's assassination

Turning Point USA's national field director, Crystal Clanton, sent a text message saying 'i hate black people. Like f— them all... I hate blacks. End of story.' and Charlie Kirk praised Clanton as 'the best hire we ever could have made'
Charlie Kirk was not allowed to speak at Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School
Charlie Kirk blamed Chicago gun violence on 'a lack of father problem in the Black community'
Charlie Kirk said Jewish money was ruining US culture
Charlie Kirk made claims about the threat from coronavirus that were tracked by The New York Times
Charlie Kirk spread coronavirus misinformation, including a flawed comparison of coronavirus and H1N1 emergency timelines
Charlie Kirk was recorded in a closed-door session saying 'Be not afraid of the accusations that you're a voter suppressor'
Jerry Falwell Jr. spent millions on pro-Trump causes during his final years at Liberty University, where Charlie Kirk was a key figure.
Charlie Kirk clashed with Geraldo Rivera over voter fraud, with Rivera calling Kirk's claims 'so dishonest'
Charlie Kirk's TPUSA launched its Faith Initiative, which partners with churches to host religious conferences and has been described as promoting Christian nationalism
Charlie Kirk was involved in the culture wars over masks in US schools, which became a 'perfect battleground' for vicious culture wars
Charlie Kirk referred to George Floyd as a 'scumbag'
Turning Point USA, led by Charlie Kirk, sought $43 million to escalate the right's culture war in American schools.
Charlie Kirk used race to reach Gen Z, according to a report.
Charlie Kirk visited Springfield and made comments about immigration and climate change
Charlie Kirk stated that Joe Biden should be 'put in prison and/or given the death penalty for crimes against America'
Charlie Kirk was described as a Christian nationalist by Kyle Spencer in his book 'Raising Them Right'
Charlie Kirk helped oust Ronna McDaniel at the RNC.
Charlie Kirk pushed conspiracies about election fraud, Christian nationalism, anti-immigrant xenophobia, and racism, including attacks on Martin Luther King
Charlie Kirk shared a stage with a right-wing activist who discussed stoning gay people to death
Charlie Kirk spoke at the RNC as a far-right conservative talk show host
Charlie Kirk was described as the new faith-focused enforcer of Trumpism by Religion News Service
Charlie Kirk accused Rep. Jasmine Crockett of being part of an 'attempt to eliminate the white population in this country'
Charlie Kirk said he would want a 10-year-old daughter who was raped to deliver the baby
Charlie Kirk used his platforms to decry racial equity programs and float conspiracy theories, which were cast as racist, xenophobic, and extreme by groups that study hate speech
Charlie Kirk called for a 'patriot' to bail out Paul Pelosi assailant David DePape.
Charlie Kirk expressed views on 'prowling blacks' and gun control in his own words
Right-wing activists and members of the Trump administration demanded that people allegedly celebrating Kirk's death be silenced and fired
Charlie Kirk railed against transgender rights, further fueling the fight
Over 100 people, including lawyers, doctors, and teachers, had been censured, suspended, dismissed, or were under investigation for expressing criticism of Charlie Kirk or celebrating his death
Charlie Kirk's Twitter account was locked for spreading misinformation about mail-in votes.
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed there is no evidence of global warming and scientists don't know the cause in a viral Turning Point USA video.
Charlie Kirk warned that Democrats want Americans to live in 'sexual anarchy'
Charlie Kirk warned on YouTube that gay people 'want to corrupt your children'
A Turning Point USA conference suggested that the role of young women leaders is to get married and have babies
Charlie Kirk likened concern about climate change to 'pseudo-paganism'.
Charlie Kirk wanted to start a border militia to protect 'white demographics in America'
Charlie Kirk called Ketanji Brown Jackson a 'recipient of affirmative action' who is 'unqualified' for the Supreme Court
Charlie Kirk defended Elon Musk's antisemitism, stating that some of the largest financiers of left-wing anti-white causes have been Jewish Americans
Charlie Kirk planned to discredit Martin Luther King Jr. and the Civil Rights Act
Charlie Kirk attacked the growing 'myth' of Martin Luther King Jr.
Charlie Kirk claimed that undocumented immigrants are part of a scheme to replace whites with nonwhites, which is a false narrative
Charlie Kirk called for shooting and whipping migrants on the southern border, stating 'If you enter, we have lethal force, and we're willing to use it.'
Charlie Kirk stated that trans-affirming care should be banned nationwide and that Donald Trump should run on this issue
Charlie Kirk called for a Nuremberg-style trial for every gender-affirming clinic doctor
Charlie Kirk amplified a since-debunked social media rumor about Haitian immigrants in Springfield, Ohio
Charlie Kirk's MAGA movement spiraled into ugly Islamophobia after Zohran Mamdani's win, with warnings that New York 'is about to see 9/11 2.0'
MAGA erupted with Islamophobic attacks on Zohran Mamdani
Charlie Kirk stated that the death toll from Texas flooding would not have been as high if it wasn't for Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiatives
Charlie Kirk stated 'I don't love the idea of sending arms to Ukraine'
The far-right responded to Charlie Kirk's shooting with calls for violence
Charlie Kirk was killed, and in response, Trump blamed the 'radical left' for his death before a suspect was identified
Some Republicans used the opportunity to condemn liberal beliefs as evil after Kirk's death
Right-wing activists targeted people for allegedly celebrating Charlie Kirk's death
Charlie Kirk's anti-LGBTQ+ controversies were explored, including calls for violence
Charlie Kirk referred to 'prowling Blacks' and 'the great replacement strategy', promoting replacement theory rhetoric
Charlie Kirk stated that the Civil Rights Act 'created a beast, and that beast has now turned into an anti-white weapon'
Charlie Kirk once said Jewish money was ruining US culture
Charlie Kirk once said prominent Black women didn't have 'brain processing power' to be taken seriously
The White House planned a broad crackdown on liberal groups in response to Kirk's death
The Trump administration said it would target far-left groups for Kirk's assassination, despite prosecutors making no such link
Over 100 people faced consequences for remarks about Charlie Kirk's death
Tucker Carlson said the Trump administration was using Charlie Kirk's killing to trample the First Amendment
The US right claimed free speech was sacred until the Charlie Kirk killing, sparking criticism of hypocrisy
